places.name: Clarksdale Crossroads
places.description: Giant novelty guitars now mark the spot where Robert Johnson is said to have sold his soul to the devil.

places.name: Muddy Waters’ Cabin
places.description: The centerpiece of the Delta Blues Museum is a wooden sharecropper’s cabin that once housed a titan of blues music.

places.name: Abe’s Bar-B-Q
places.description: Steps from the site where Robert Johnson purportedly sold his soul to the Devil, you'll find this century-old barbecue joint.

places.name: Cat Head Delta Blues & Folk Art
places.description: Swing by this iconic record store find out what’s going on in Clarksdale’s live music scene.

places.name: Red’s
places.description: Pay your respects at this juke joint founded by a blues legend of the Mississippi Delta.
